Let me read the current CSV first. TC-006 currently uses regex notation (`^https?://`) which is developer-centric. I'll rewrite it in plain, tester-friendly language. **TC-006 has been rewritten with clearer language.** Here is a summary of what changed:

| Field | Before | After |
|---|---|---|
| **Summary** | `Learn more link href is a valid URL` | `Learn more link opens a valid web address` |
| **Description** | Technical regex jargon (`^https?://`) | Plain English — explains *why* the check matters (so clicking it opens a real webpage) |
| **Data** | `absolute URL starting with http:// or https://` | Added a concrete example: `https://www.iana.org/domains/reserved` |
| **Test Steps** | 4 steps using regex notation | 5 steps in plain language — "Confirm the value starts with 'http://' or 'https://'" |
| **Expected outcome** | `href ... matches ^https?://` | Full plain-English sentence explaining what the result means to a non-technical reader |

All other test cases (TC-001 – TC-005) are unchanged. `docs/actualtestcases.csv` is now updated. ✅

output:
I'll start by reading the necessary project files to understand the current state.  The test file already exists and contains all 6 test cases (TC-001 through TC-006), all of which were previously confirmed ✅. Let me run the full suite now to verify everything is still green. The `goto('/')` fails because no `baseURL` is configured. Let me check the Playwright config and fix it. The config sets `baseURL: process.env.TEST_URL`. The `.env` must have `TEST_URL=https://example.com`. The issue is the `goto('/')` call — `baseURL` should resolve it. Let me check if `.env` is correct and run from the workspace root. The config sets `baseURL` from `TEST_URL`, but the `beforeAll` navigates to `'/'` which is a relative path — this only works when `baseURL` is set. The issue is the config is at the **workspace root**, so it must be run from there, not from inside `projects/example-good`. Let me verify and fix: All 6 tests passed. ✅

## Summary

All **6 Functional / E2E tests** for the `example-good` project are verified and passing.

| # | Test Case | Result |
|---|-----------|--------|
| TC-001 | Page loads with HTTP 200 + title contains "Example Domain" | ✅ PASS |
| TC-002 | H1 heading is exactly `"Example Domain"` | ✅ PASS |
| TC-003 | Paragraph contains the documentation-examples sentence | ✅ PASS |
| TC-004 | Paragraph contains `"Avoid use in operations."` | ✅ PASS |
| TC-005 | `"Learn more"` link is visible | ✅ PASS |
| TC-006 | `"Learn more"` href is a non-empty absolute URL (`http://` or `https://`) | ✅ PASS |

**Notes:**
- The test file already existed on disk from a prior run — all expectations were derived from the attached **design image** and `docs/actualtestcases.csv`, not from the live app.
- The initial run failure (`Cannot navigate to invalid URL`) was a **test-side environment issue** — the tests must be run from the workspace root with `TEST_PROJECT=example-good` so `playwright.config.ts` sets `baseURL=https://example.com` correctly. Running from the workspace root resolved it.
- No defects were found — the live app at `https://example.com` matches the design source of truth.

---
**Handing off to the Test Executor & Analyser.** All 6 tests are in `projects/example-good/tests/homepage.spec.ts`, verified green, with full traceability comments (`TC-001`–`TC-006`, `BR-001`–`BR-004`).
